warnings: Asset size limit warnings (editor.js 337 KiB) - expected for theme bundles

syncStatus.notes: TSX and PHP structure fully verified after all three changes. Both files now use: (1) sprintf pattern for author/date, (2) loop over all categories for pills, (3) first category accent color for section gradient override. Conditional classes and element structure match exactly.

fixesApplied.description: Use sprintf for author and date strings instead of inline concatenation
fixesApplied.action: PHP: Changed author line from `esc_html__('By:', 'takt') . ' ' . esc_html($postAuthor)` to `esc_html(sprintf(__('By: %s', 'takt'), $postAuthor))`. Same pattern for date line with `sprintf(__('Published on: %s', 'takt'), $postDate)`. Both have translators comments. TSX: Imported `sprintf` from `@wordpress/i18n` and changed both lines to use `sprintf(__('By: %s', 'takt'), postAuthor)` and `sprintf(__('Published on: %s', 'takt'), postDate)`. This makes the full string translatable as a single unit.

fixesApplied.description: Pills should list ALL topics, not just the first one
fixesApplied.action: PHP: Replaced single `$firstCategory` pill with a `foreach ($categories as $category)` loop inside a `<div class='flex flex-wrap gap-2'>` container. Each pill gets its own accent color via `get_term_meta($category->term_id, 'accent_color', true)`. TSX: Replaced single `firstCategory` pill with `allCategories.map()` inside a `<div className='flex flex-wrap gap-2'>`. Each pill gets its accent color from the `categoryAccentColors` record keyed by category ID. The `useSelect` hook now fetches accent colors for ALL categories in a single selector. The section gradient still uses the first category's accent color (via `$firstAccentColor` / `firstAccentColor`) when useTopicColor is true.

fixesApplied.description: Investigate and fix repeating $showExcerpt and $useTopicColor variables in PHP
fixesApplied.action: Investigation: The previous FQA-001 fix changed `$attributes['showExcerpt'] ?? false` to `$showExcerpt ?? false`, which is still a redundant re-declaration. The theme's `inc/helpers/blocks.php` runs `extract($attributes, EXTR_SKIP)` before including PHP templates, which automatically creates `$showExcerpt` and `$useTopicColor` from the block.json attribute definitions (with their defaults). The `?? false` lines were therefore declaring the same variables that already existed. Fix: Removed both lines entirely (`$showExcerpt = $showExcerpt ?? false;` and `$useTopicColor = $useTopicColor ?? false;`). The auto-extracted variables from block.json attributes are used directly throughout the template. The @var docblock already documents these variables for PHP_CodeSniffer.
